rationale: No concrete ordinary-flow fail-open beyond the three disclosed residuals was found. R1 fail-closes epic lookup, closed-child enumeration, and ancestry errors; its intentional bool probe ambiguity is covered for merge candidates by the independently fail-closed Option-B leg over the same bd worktree source FinalizeEpic uses. R2 is decoration only and cannot reverse refusal. R3 fail-closes plan enumeration, metadata reads, present-null/wrong-type values, and invalid slot/round shapes, validating both pending and covering arrays before early return. It is reached before phase, epic-close, merge, or push mutations, and panel hatches are not consulted. MergeBase provides the whole-ref-store pre-scan abort; FinalizeEpic only merges real enumerated bead worktree branches. First-orphan-only reporting is safe because any refusal prevents all terminal work. C+B holds: BranchExistsE and internal Go show-ref searches are zero-hit, and internal/gitutil is unchanged from origin/main. go build ./... and the requested uncached approve/complete/lifecycle/executor tests passed. The raw-merged/no-obligation, cross-spec global-loop, and between-two-List concurrent-mutation residuals are accurately bounded and do not conceal a broader same-spec serialized-flow bypass.

findings.finding: Whole-gate same-spec serialized flow blocks every worktree-enumerated, unmerged closed epic bead before FinalizeEpic.
Durable-obligation decoding and coverage are fail-closed for read failures, present-null/corrupt values, and invalid element shapes.
C+B structural invariant and MergeBase/FinalizeEpic supporting facts hold branch-wide.

Three explicitly disclosed limits remain: raw-git-merged beads without obligations, cross-spec worktrees in the global finalize loop, and concurrent mutation between the gate and finalize worktree enumerations; each is honestly scoped and is not a new in-scope fail-open.
